title: Policies and Market Development to Accelerate Technological Change in the Semiarid Zones: A Focus on Sub-Saharan Africa
abstract:
During the past two decades (1980–2000) donors from developed countries have
been promoting structural adjustment in developing countries. This generally includes
getting the public sector out of an active role in the economy, moving
toward an open or free-trade regime, and eliminating subsidies (Stiglitz, 2002).
Policy options, such as marketing boards and input subsidies, have been discouraged,
if not eliminated, as available policy tools at the disposal of most
developing countries......
